corrections.text: The article states the release 'patches 25 security vulnerabilities—4 rated high severity' (appearing in both the summary and Overview section). Mozilla's security advisory MFSA2026-46 (https://www.mozilla.org/en-US/security/advisories/mfsa2026-46/) lists 31 CVEs in total (29 affecting desktop Firefox, 2 affecting Firefox for Android/Firefox Focus only), with 5 high-severity CVEs affecting desktop Firefox: CVE-2026-8945, CVE-2026-8946, CVE-2026-8947, CVE-2026-8948, and CVE-2026-8975. The figure '25' does not appear in any cited source.

The article attributes the phrase 'evidence of potential exploitation' (inside quotation marks) to the memory-safety CVEs CVE-2026-8973, CVE-2026-8974, and CVE-2026-8975. The advisory's actual language is: 'Some of these bugs showed evidence of memory corruption and we presume that with enough effort some of these could have been exploited to run arbitrary code.' The phrase 'evidence of potential exploitation' does not appear verbatim in the advisory.
